column1: # Constraint: A (every claim attributed to a woman in elected office; varied office levels)
claim_text
Senator Maria Chen voted against an amendment that would have stripped funding from refugee resettlement programs.
Representative Tashia Williams introduced legislation expanding fentanyl test strip distribution in 12 federal districts.
Governor Linnea Hartwell signed a bill capping insulin copays at $35 per month for state employees.
AG Priya Venkatesan filed federal charges against a hospital executive in a $94 million Medicare fraud scheme.
Mayor Imani Brooks announced that the city would expand its early childhood education program to 4,800 additional children.
State Senator Rosa Delgado co-sponsored legislation requiring background checks at all federally licensed gun shows.
Representative Yuki Nakamura voted to extend Pell grant eligibility to short-term workforce credentialing programs.
Governor Adaeze Onuoha vetoed a bill that would have eliminated state Medicaid coverage for emergency dental care.
Councilwoman Sarah Levenson introduced an ordinance requiring landlords to disclose rent-stabilization status at lease signing.
Senator Beatrice Holloway voted to reauthorize the Violence Against Women Act with expanded tribal jurisdiction.
Lieutenant Governor Mireille Boucher launched a state task force on prescription drug affordability for rural seniors.
Representative Genevieve Park co-sponsored legislation expanding visa quotas for nurses serving rural counties.
Mayor Esperanza Quintero announced a $32 million investment in violence interruption programs across four neighborhoods.
Senator Catalina Ruiz voted against a constitutional amendment requiring two-thirds majorities for federal tax increases.
Treasurer Anjali Shah announced that the state's pension fund had divested $1.4 billion from fossil fuel holdings.
Governor Tova Lindqvist signed legislation tying state university funding to graduation rates for first-generation students.
AG Adelaide Pemberton announced a settlement requiring three opioid manufacturers to pay $640 million to her state.
Representative Mei-Lin Cho introduced a bill creating a federal database of police use-of-force incidents.
State Representative Hannah Goldenberg voted to expand free school meals to all K-12 students statewide.
Senator Sarmistha Banerjee co-sponsored legislation expanding telehealth coverage for Medicare beneficiaries permanently.
Mayor Olamide Adesanya announced that the city would phase out cash bail for misdemeanor offenses by year end.
Comptroller Yelena Petrova projected a $2.1 billion budget surplus for the upcoming fiscal year.
Governor Cheyenne Stillwater vetoed a bill that would have expanded the state's voucher program to all income levels.
Senator Daria Mironov voted to extend the federal Earned Income Tax Credit expansion through 2028.
Representative Folake Adebayo introduced legislation requiring colleges to disclose campus sexual assault statistics annually.
AG Marisol Castaneda filed civil rights suits against three jails for failing to provide adequate mental health treatment.
Mayor Tabitha Aronson announced a partnership with the local hospital to provide trauma-informed care training for educators.
State Senator Inara Khoury voted against a measure that would have privatized state-run psychiatric hospitals.
Representative Soledad Vega co-sponsored a bill expanding green card eligibility for healthcare workers in shortage areas.
Governor Aiyana Crow announced an executive order halting state cooperation with federal immigration detainer requests.
Senator Charlotte Beauchamp introduced legislation funding workforce retraining programs in coal communities.
State Treasurer Penelope Dietrich reported that the state pension fund returned 8.4 percent over the past fiscal year.
Mayor Hina Yamamoto announced that the city would expand free preschool to 2,200 additional three-year-olds.
Representative Ofelia Ramirez voted against a bill that would have eliminated DACA protections for current recipients.
Governor Ingrid Solberg signed a bill establishing a $400 million venture fund for women-owned small businesses.
Senator Jamila Toure introduced legislation creating tax credits for employers who provide on-site childcare.
AG Eleni Papadopoulos announced a probe into algorithmic bias in healthcare claim denials by major insurers.
State Representative Sigrid Bjornson voted to expand federal grants for school-based mental health services.
Councilwoman Aisha Rahman introduced a resolution declaring the city a safe haven for reproductive healthcare access.
Governor Esperanza Cabrera vetoed legislation that would have eliminated the state's earned income tax credit.
Senator Wren Halloway co-sponsored a bill expanding federal drug pricing transparency requirements.
Representative Saoirse O'Brien voted to extend the child tax credit and expand its refundability.
Mayor Carmen Aragon announced a citywide initiative providing free SAT preparation to all public high school juniors.
AG Vivienne Lemoine filed criminal charges against three landlords for systematically excluding voucher holders.
State Senator Theodora Kapp voted against funding additional state correctional facilities in her district.
Governor Beatrix Ferdinand signed legislation expanding paid family leave to all private-sector workers statewide.
Representative Magnolia Pierce introduced a bill creating a federal grant for rural hospital workforce retention.
Senator Camille Foreman voted to advance legislation banning solitary confinement for individuals under 21.
Mayor Ottilie Vance announced that the city would invest $18 million in refurbishing aging school HVAC systems.
State Representative Ngozi Eze voted to expand the state's Medicaid coverage for postpartum care to one full year.
AG Bronwen Llewellyn announced indictments in a $48 million wage theft case affecting 2,200 hospitality workers.
Governor Hadley Sterling signed a bill increasing state per-pupil funding by $740 million over four years.
Senator Annika Sundberg co-sponsored legislation requiring Medicare to negotiate prices for the next 50 highest-cost drugs.
Representative Lakshmi Iyer voted against an amendment that would have eliminated Title IX funding enforcement.
Comptroller Renata Voss projected that state debt service would consume 7.8 percent of general fund revenue next year.
Mayor Chiamaka Okeke announced a partnership with three community colleges to expand healthcare workforce training.
State Senator Polina Karolinska voted to expand the state's renewable energy tax credit through 2030.
Governor Mariko Tanaka-Reeves signed legislation tying corporate tax incentives to apprenticeship hiring quotas.
AG Rosalind Whittaker filed antitrust complaints against three pharmacy benefit managers for anticompetitive rebate practices.
Senator Delphine Aubrey voted to advance legislation creating a federal commission on housing affordability.
Representative Kalinda Sutter introduced a bill expanding refundable adoption tax credits for foster families.
Mayor Astrid Halvorsen announced that the city had reduced unsheltered homelessness by 22 percent over two years.
Governor Esme Carradine vetoed a bill that would have expanded ICE access to state driver's license databases.
Senator Quinn Whitfield co-sponsored legislation funding the construction of 12 new federal courthouses nationwide.
Representative Beatriz Castellanos voted to extend Temporary Protected Status for Central American countries through 2027.
AG Aida Gallagher announced a settlement with three for-profit colleges totaling $128 million in student debt relief.
Lieutenant Governor Solange Ardoin announced a state initiative providing free tax preparation services in 84 counties.
State Representative Phoebe Carlisle voted against a bill that would have privatized state public defender services.
Mayor Naima Diop announced that the city would expand its second-chance hiring program to all municipal departments.
Governor Lior Aviram signed legislation expanding mental health insurance parity requirements for state-regulated plans.
Senator Haley Westerlund introduced a bill creating federal grants for evidence-based teacher mentoring programs.
Representative Ximena Aldana voted to expand the federal Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act funding by 18 percent.
AG Constance Whitehorse filed federal civil rights charges against three deputies in a custody death investigation.
Mayor Sumaya Hassan announced a citywide expansion of community health workers serving immigrant neighborhoods.
State Senator Briallen Pritchard voted against a measure that would have eliminated state-funded preschool slots.
Governor Frieda Halverson signed legislation funding 1,400 new affordable housing units in three urban centers.
